product_updates.title: As OpenAI files for IPO, Sam Altman’s eye-scanning company is doing layoffs, report says
product_updates.content: Tools for Humanity, Sam Altman's identity verification company, is reportedly struggling to generate revenue and will downsize its staff.

product_updates.title: Apple’s WWDC AI demos looked more real after $250M false ad settlement
product_updates.content: The vibe of Apple's 2026 WWDC keynote felt like a spouse proudly listing all the honey-do-list items tackled. One subtle example: the many AI demos of someone standing, phone in hand.

product_updates.title: OpenAI files confidentially for IPO, following Anthropic
product_updates.content: The filing comes a little more than a week after its main rival, Anthropic, also filed to go public, ramping up the race between the two AI firms.

product_updates.title: Apple plays catch-up at WWDC
product_updates.content: Apple spent much of its WWDC keynote highlighting fixes, performance improvements, and long-requested features before unveiling its upgraded AI-powered Siri, signaling that the company wants users to see AI as just one part of a broader effort to improve its software.

product_updates.title: Apple bets cheaper AI will woo small developers
product_updates.content: As AI experimentation grows more expensive, Apple is waiving cloud API costs for developers with fewer than 2 million first-time App Store downloads.

product_updates.title: WWDC 2026: Everything announced on Siri AI, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence and more
product_updates.content: Apple primarily made the case for an improved experience with its longstanding Siri assistant, which like most other announcements had a hefty helping of AI.

product_updates.title: Apple just taught your iPhone to finish your sentences, your photos, and your workflows
product_updates.content: Apple is adding new AI-powered features to Safari, Shortcuts, and Password apps.

product_updates.title: Apple will let you build workflows using AI in its new Shortcuts app
product_updates.content: Shortcuts gets an AI upgrade, letting you describe the workflow you want in a prompt.

product_updates.title: Apple’s Image Playground doesn’t suck anymore
product_updates.content: Apple's AI image generator is getting a makeover that could make it more competitive.

product_updates.title: Apple’s Photos app is getting new AI editing features
product_updates.content: A new spatial "Reframe" feature will let users use AI to adjust perspectives.

technical_breakthroughs.title: For the 2nd time in weeks, Microsoft packages laced with credential stealer
technical_breakthroughs.content: 73 packages run self-replicating stealer as soon as they're opened by an AI agent.

technical_breakthroughs.title: The Download: how the World Cup ball will fly and OpenAI’s “super app”
technical_breakthroughs.content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Why this year’s World Cup ball may not fly as far Much is new about this month’s FIFA World Cup tournament. It hosts more teams than ever before. It’s the first…

technical_breakthroughs.title: Why this year’s World Cup ball may not fly as far
technical_breakthroughs.content: Much is new about this month’s upcoming FIFA World Cup tournament, which will be held in the US, Canada, and Mexico. It hosts more teams than ever before. It’s the first to occur in three different host countries. And, like predecessor cups for over half a century, it will employ a soccer ball wi…
